corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: Our progressive attention process with local context and hard attention mechanism consistently achieves the best weighted mAP scores in both experimental settings as shown in Table (left).
We observe that our progressive attention models outperform the baselines and become stronger when incoporated with hard attention.
We also present the attention quality of the models using true-positive ratio (TPR) in Table (left).
The baseline networks also share the same architecture of CNN of VGG-16 network as in PAN (Figure ).
All models are evaluated via mean average precision (mAP) weighted by the frequencies of the attribute labels in test set, where the computation of mAP follows PASCAL VOC protocol .
Figure presents qualitative results on VG dataset, which show that the progressive attention models gradually attend to target objects.
We propose a novel attention model that can accurately attends to target objects of various scales and shapes in images.
The experiments on synthetic and real datasets show that the proposed attention networks outperform traditional attention methods in visual attribute prediction tasks.

Many visual attention models have been introduced in the previous literature, and they have shown that attaching an attention to NNs improves the accuracy in various tasks such as image classification , image generation , image caption generation and visual question answering .
To address this type of tasks, the network architecture should incorporate an attentive mechanism either explicitly or implicitly.

To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel attention network, referred to as progressive attention network (PAN), which enables precise attention over objects of different scales and shapes by attaching attentive mechanisms to multiple layers within a convolutional neural network (CNN).

To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel attention network, referred to as progressive attention network (PAN), which enables precise attention over objects of different scales and shapes by attaching attentive mechanisms to multiple layers within a convolutional neural network (CNN).
We then present our experimental results on several datasets in Section 4 and conclude the paper in Section 5.

The proposed network has advantage of end-to-end trainability by a standard backpropagation without any extra technique. clearly outperforms other approaches with significant margins and PAN[ * ]+CTX further improves performance by exploiting local context for attention estimation.
Our progressive attention model with the hard attention process and local context achieves the best performance in most cases.
Figure illustrates qualitative results of the two proposed methods and two baselines on MBG dataset.
The architecture of image encoding networks in SAN and HAN, and localization networks in STNs are all identical for fair comparisons.
The CNN in the proposed network also has the same architecture except for the additional layers for progressive attention.

While progressive attention networks can be implemented with either soft or hard attention, we demonstrated that both versions of the model substantially outperform existing methods on both synthetic and real datasets.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
